title: Quantum geometry induced anomalous chiral transport and hidden symmetry breaking in centrosymmetric 2M-WS2
authors: Hang Cui, Shao-Bo Liu, Erqing Wang, Mingxiang Pan, Yuqiang Fang, Ning Ma, Wenlong Liu, Di Chen, Yu Zhang, Yuanjun Song, Tingting Hao, Jiankun Li, Jian Cui, Ya Feng, Haiwen Liu, Fuqiang Huang, Huaqing Huang, X. -C. Xie, Jian-Hao Chen
abstract: Chirality, a widely existing material property in nature involving the breaking of the left-right symmetry, has profound influences in various fields of natural sciences. Nonlinear response, such as electronic magnetochiral anisotropy (eMChA), has been recognized as a sensitive probe for the effects of symmetry breaking and nontrivial quantum geometries in solids. So far, observations of eMChA have primarily been limited to inversion-symmetry broken materials. Here, we report a remarkable chiral transport in centrosymmetric candidate topological superconductor 2M-WS2 flakes observed via second-harmonic generation under an out-of-plane magnetic field. More importantly, the eMChA becomes significant around the crossover temperature TFL ~ 25 K from the Fermi liquid (FL) to strange metal (SM) in the normal state, which interestingly echoes with the anomalously large Nernst response at the same temperature in bulk 2M-WS2. These observations reveal a direct correspondence between the nonlinear response, Nernst response, and FL-SM transition in 2M-WS2. Theoretical analysis indicates that nontrivial quantum geometry is behind the simultaneous response of eMChA and Nernst effects in 2M-WS2 and the contribution from the orbital magnetic moment at the Fermi surface becomes significant during the FL-SM transition. Based on first-principles calculations, a thick-layer-sliding mechanism with minimal energy gain in 2M-WS2 provides one possibility for the generation of such nontrivial quantum geometry. The intertwined physics of remarkable eMChA, Nernst response, and FL-SM transition make 2M-WS2 a rare quantum platform to study the chiral transport and unexplored phenomena in strange metals, which may shed light on the trans-century, unresolved scientific issue in unconventional high-temperature superconductivity.
summary: This paper reports the discovery of unexpected chiral transport in the centrosymmetric material 2M-WS2. The researchers show that this nonlinear response, along with a large Nernst effect, emerges simultaneously with a transition from a Fermi liquid to a strange metal. These phenomena are attributed to nontrivial quantum geometry and potential hidden symmetry breaking.
main_problem: Investigating the origin of unexpected nonreciprocal transport (eMChA) and hidden symmetry breaking in the centrosymmetric material 2M-WS2, and understanding its connection to the Fermi liquid to strange metal transition.
main_result: The discovery of remarkable electronic magnetochiral anisotropy (eMChA) in centrosymmetric 2M-WS2 that coincides with an anomalous Nernst response and a crossover from Fermi liquid to strange metal at 25 K, driven by nontrivial quantum geometry.
method: Experimental transport measurements using second-harmonic generation (SHG) and lock-in techniques, combined with first-principles DFT calculations and phenomenological modeling of nonreciprocal transport.
model_or_system: The physical system is 2M-WS2, a metastable, centrosymmetric candidate topological superconductor characterized by a crossover from Fermi liquid to strange metal behavior.
key_observables: Second-harmonic voltage (V2w), first-harmonic voltage (V1w), eMChA coefficient (gamma), Nernst coefficient (nu), scattering rate (Gamma_tr), and Hall coefficient (RH).
important_parameters: Crossover temperature TFL approx 25 K, superconducting transition temperature Tc = 8.8 K, and magnetic field (B) and current (I) amplitudes.
main_assumptions: The existence of a thick-layer-sliding mechanism as a source for nontrivial quantum geometry.
figures_summary: Fig 1: Concept of symmetry breaking and nonreciprocal transport; Fig 2: 2M-WS2 device geometry, symmetry, and voltage dependence on B and I; Fig 3: Angular, temperature, and field dependence of eMChA; Fig 4: Phase diagram correlating eMChA, Nernst effect, and scattering rates.
paper_structure: Introduction to symmetry breaking and eMChA; Experimental setup and device fabrication; Observation of eMChA in 2M-WS2; Correlation of eMChA with Nernst effect and FL-SM transition; Theoretical analysis of quantum geometry and structural origin; Conclusion.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
